title: Spring is Here — and So Are the Density Altitude Warnings
body: With temps climbing into the 70s this week, density altitude at 18V is already nudging 6,500 ft on warm afternoons. If you're flying a heavier gross weight or a fixed-pitch prop, plan your departures for early morning when the air is cool and the runway feels 4,100 ft again. Perfect grass strip weather, though — the 9/27 is in great shape.

title: Pancake Breakfast Fly-In — April 11
body: First fly-in of 2026 is right around the corner. We're firing up the griddle on April 11th, 8–10 AM on the ramp. Fly in, drive in, or wander over from your hangar — all welcome. $10 suggested donation, kids fly free. CTAF 122.9, easy pattern, plenty of tiedowns.

title: Fuel at $6.35/gal — Still the Best Price on the Front Range
body: We updated 100LL to $6.35/gal. Self-serve, 24/7 credit card, no line. Compare that to $7.50+ at KBJC and KAPA and the math is pretty clear. Tell your flying club.

title: Tailwheel Season is Open
body: The grass strip is waking up and so are the taildraggers. The 9/27 turf is firm and mowed — perfect for getting those three-point landings dialed in before fly-in season kicks off. If you've been thinking about tailwheel training, there's no better place on the Front Range to do it.

title: Coffee & Donuts — First Friday of Every Month
body: It's simple: show up, pour a cup, eat a donut, talk airplanes. First Friday of every month, early morning on the ramp. No agenda, no registration, no fee. Just the regulars and whoever decides to drop in. Bring a lawn chair if you want to stay a while.

title: Front Range Mountain Wave — Know Before You Go
body: Spring wind events have been coming through weekly this year. When the westerlies kick up above 30 kts at ridge level, expect rotor turbulence between 5,000–9,000 ft west of I-25. 18V sits east of the foothills so the ramp stays calm, but climb-out to the west can get sporty. Check your PIREPS and give yourself extra fuel for the return.

title: Hangar Availability — Get on the List
body: We occasionally have T-hangar and shade hangar openings as the season turns over. If you're tired of fighting the elements with a tiedown, now's the time to get your name in. Drop us a message through the contact form and we'll reach out when something comes available.

title: 60+ Years of Keeping the Pattern Alive
body: Platte Valley Airpark has been welcoming pilots since the early 1960s. That's six decades of predawn preflight checks, first solos, and hangar flying sessions that ran way too long. We're proud to still be here — and proud of every pilot who calls 18V home.
